h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.anchor_quote: [slides the arrow key next to the marked worksheet] On the key, grass -> rabbit means energy goes from food to eater, so rabbit -> hawk is hawk eating rabbit, not chasing.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I can put 2 in both of these, but then it looks like I missed the point of having two labels.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would keep negotiating the visible sameness of the two 2s until they agreed it was fine with labels, without stopping to re-examine their own earlier action of placing the pencil across both blanks.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Make the sticker answer this first: 2 of what? Point to the label before it goes back. If it is side scale, name the one side length. If it is perimeter scale, name the whole border total. Only then put the 2 in the blank.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: In response to L2's persistent discomfort with identical-looking 2s, the tutor breaks the blank-filling habit by introducing a ritualized object-and-label interrogation sequence that forces measurement naming before placement.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Keep the sticker there until the label answers: 2 of what? Side scale: one side length, 3 to 6. Perimeter scale: whole border total, 12 to 24. Area scale: 4 square units, 2 across times 2 up. Now the blank cannot hide the job.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: After the learner's pivot re-frames the blank as hiding two jobs, the tutor extends the sticker-pause ritual into a permanent 'blank cannot hide the job' staging, directly ratifying and institutionalizing the learner's revised frame.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I can put 2 in both of these, but then it looks like I missed the point of having two labels.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Keep the sticker there until the label answers: 2 of what? Side scale: one side length, 3 to 6. Perimeter scale: whole border total, 12 to 24. Area scale: 4 square units, 2 across times 2 up. Now the blank cannot hide the job.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: After the learner's pivot re-frames the blank as hiding two jobs, the tutor extends the sticker-pause ritual into a permanent 'blank cannot hide the job' staging, directly ratifying and institutionalizing the learner's revised frame.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Make the sticker answer this first: 2 of what? Point to the label before it goes back. If it is side scale, name the one side length. If it is perimeter scale, name the whole border total. Only then put the 2 in the blank.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: In response to L2's persistent discomfort with identical-looking 2s, the tutor breaks the blank-filling habit by introducing a ritualized object-and-label interrogation sequence that forces measurement naming before placement.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Make the sticker answer this first: 2 of what? Point to the label before it goes back. If it is side scale, name the one side length. If it is perimeter scale, name the whole border total. Only then put the 2 in the blank.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: In response to L2's persistent discomfort with identical-looking 2s, the tutor breaks the blank-filling habit by introducing a ritualized object-and-label interrogation sequence that forces measurement naming before placement.

scored.recoheredEarlier: This is double the side, so I still want to call it double.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to reluctantly accept the area factor while lamenting the initial mistake, rather than actively reframing the original claim as partially correct.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep the dislike; it is not in charge of the count. ... If the side factor were 3, what area factor would the same grid logic force?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from explaining to engaging the learner in a predictive generalization, moving from addressing emotional resistance to analytical reasoning.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: “Double” is restricted, not erased. It belongs to the side mark only. ... Old side 5, new side 10. State the area factor without using the word “double.”
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor adopts the learner's reframing of 'double' and designs a task that eliminates that word, reinforcing the restricted meaning.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: This is double the side, so I still want to call it double.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: “Double” is restricted, not erased. It belongs to the side mark only. ... Old side 5, new side 10. State the area factor without using the word “double.”
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor adopts the learner's reframing of 'double' and designs a task that eliminates that word, reinforcing the restricted meaning.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Keep the dislike; it is not in charge of the count. ... If the side factor were 3, what area factor would the same grid logic force?
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts from explaining to engaging the learner in a predictive generalization, moving from addressing emotional resistance to analytical reasoning.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Keep the dislike; it is not in charge of the count. ... If the side factor were 3, what area factor would the same grid logic force?
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor shifts from explaining to engaging the learner in a predictive generalization, moving from addressing emotional resistance to analytical reasoning.
